title: Velvety Salty Spanish Chicken Paella
description: A onepot paella bursting with salty flavors, wild garlic leaves, and tender sousvide chicken, high in protein and comforting for dinner.

ingredients.name: boneless skinless chicken thighs
ingredients.notes: cut into 2.5 cm cubes for sous‑vide

instructions: Preheat a sous‑vide bath to 65 °C (149 °F). Season the chicken cubes with salt, pepper and smoked paprika, seal in a vacuum bag, and cook for 1 h 30 min.
Meanwhile, steep the saffron strands in 50 ml of warm water for 10 min to release their color and flavor.
In a large paella or sauté pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 min.
Add the minced garlic and wild garlic leaves, sauté for 30 sec until fragrant.
Stir in the rice, coating it with oil and letting it toast lightly for 1 min.
Deglaze the pan with the dry white wine, letting it reduce for 2 min.
Add the saffron infusion and the chicken stock. Bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 18–20 min, or until the rice is al dente and the liquid absorbed.
Remove the cooked chicken from the bag, slice or shred it, and return it to the pan. Stir everything together and heat through for 2 min.
Taste, adjust seasoning with additional salt or pepper if needed, then serve hot.
